heading: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation
label: 1315a Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation

Construction Regarding Telehealth Services, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–10, title I, § 101(e)(5)129 Stat. 122Apr. 16, 2015“Nothing in the provisions of, or amendments made by, this title [see Tables for classification] shall be construed as precluding an alternative payment model or a qualifying APM participant (as those terms are defined in section 1833(z) of the Social Security Act [(z)], as added by paragraph (1)) from furnishing a telehealth service for which payment is not made under section 1834(m) of the Social Security Act ().”42 U.S.C. 139542 U.S.C. 1395m(m)l

Medicaid Global Payment System Demonstration Project, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–148, title II, § 2705124 Stat. 324Mar. 23, 2010“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the ‘Secretary’) shall, in coordination with the Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (as established under section 1115A of the Social Security Act [], as added by section 3021 of this Act), establish the Medicaid Global Payment System Demonstration Project under which a participating State shall adjust the payments made to an eligible safety net hospital system or network from a fee-for-service payment structure to a global capitated payment model.42 U.S.C. 1315a“(b).—Duration and ScopeThe demonstration project conducted under this section shall operate during a period of fiscal years 2010 through 2012. The Secretary shall select not more than 5 States to participate in the demonstration project.“(c).—Eligible Safety Net Hospital System or NetworkFor purposes of this section, the term ‘eligible safety net hospital system or network’ means a large, safety net hospital system or network (as defined by the Secretary) that operates within a State selected by the Secretary under subsection (b).“(d)Evaluation.—“(1).—TestingThe Innovation Center shall test and evaluate the demonstration project conducted under this section to examine any changes in health care quality outcomes and spending by the eligible safety net hospital systems or networks.“(2).—Budget neutralityDuring the testing period under paragraph (1), any budget neutrality requirements under section 1115A(b)(3) of the Social Security Act [] (as so added) shall not be applicable.42 U.S.C. 1315a(b)(3)“(3).—ModificationDuring the testing period under paragraph (1), the Secretary may, in the Secretary’s discretion, modify or terminate the demonstration project conducted under this section.“(e).—ReportNot later than 12 months after the date of completion of the demonstration project under this section, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report containing the results of the evaluation and testing conducted under subsection (d), together with recommendations for such legislation and administrative action as the Secretary determines appropriate.“(f).—Authorization of AppropriationsThere are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.”

children.heading: Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation established
children.label: a Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation established

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is created within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services a Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (in this section referred to as the “CMI”) to carry out the duties described in this section. The purpose of the CMI is to test innovative payment and service delivery models to reduce program expenditures under the applicable subchapters while preserving or enhancing the quality of care furnished to individuals under such subchapters. In selecting such models, the Secretary shall give preference to models that also improve the coordination, quality, and efficiency of health care services furnished to applicable individuals defined in paragraph (4)(A).

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 2 Deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the CMI is carrying out the duties described in this section by not later than .January 1, 2011

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 3 Consultation
children.text: In carrying out the duties under this section, the CMI shall consult representatives of relevant Federal agencies, and clinical and analytical experts with expertise in medicine and health care management. The CMI shall use open door forums or other mechanisms to seek input from interested parties.

children.label: i
children.text: an individual who is entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under part A of subchapter XVIII or enrolled for benefits under part B of such subchapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual who is eligible for medical assistance under subchapter XIX, under a State plan or waiver; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an individual who meets the criteria of both clauses (i) and (ii).

children.heading: Applicable subchapter
children.label: B Applicable subchapter
children.text: The term “applicable subchapter” means subchapter XVIII, subchapter XIX, or both.

children.heading: Testing within certain geographic areas
children.label: 5 Testing within certain geographic areas
children.text: For purposes of testing payment and service delivery models under this section, the Secretary may elect to limit testing of a model to certain geographic areas.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The CMI shall test payment and service delivery models in accordance with selection criteria under paragraph (2) to determine the effect of applying such models under the applicable subchapter (as defined in subsection (a)(4)(B)) on program expenditures under such subchapters and the quality of care received by individuals receiving benefits under such subchapter.

children.heading: Selection of models to be tested
children.label: 2 Selection of models to be tested

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall select models to be tested from models where the Secretary determines that there is evidence that the model addresses a defined population for which there are deficits in care leading to poor clinical outcomes or potentially avoidable expenditures. The Secretary shall focus on models expected to reduce program costs under the applicable subchapter while preserving or enhancing the quality of care received by individuals receiving benefits under such subchapter. The models selected under this subparagraph may include, but are not limited to, the models described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: Promoting broad payment and practice reform in primary care, including patient-centered medical home models for high-need applicable individuals, medical homes that address women’s unique health care needs, and models that transition primary care practices away from fee-for-service based reimbursement and toward comprehensive payment or salary-based payment.

children.label: ii
children.text: Contracting directly with groups of providers of services and suppliers to promote innovative care delivery models, such as through risk-based comprehensive payment or salary-based payment.

children.label: I
children.text: An inability to perform 2 or more activities of daily living.

children.label: II
children.text: Cognitive impairment, including dementia.

children.label: iv
children.text: Promote care coordination between providers of services and suppliers that transition health care providers away from fee-for-service based reimbursement and toward salary-based payment.1So in original. Probably should be “Promoting”.1

children.label: v
children.text: Supporting care coordination for chronically-ill applicable individuals at high risk of hospitalization through a health information technology-enabled provider network that includes care coordinators, a chronic disease registry, and home tele-health technology.

children.label: vi
children.text: Varying payment to physicians who order advanced diagnostic imaging services (as defined in ) according to the physician’s adherence to appropriateness criteria for the ordering of such services, as determined in consultation with physician specialty groups and other relevant stakeholders.section 1395m(e)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: Utilizing medication therapy management services, such as those described in .section 299b–35 of this title

children.label: viii
children.text: Establishing community-based health teams to support small-practice medical homes by assisting the primary care practitioner in chronic care management, including patient self-management, activities.

children.label: ix
children.text: Assisting applicable individuals in making informed health care choices by paying providers of services and suppliers for using patient decision-support tools, including tools that meet the standards developed and identified under , that improve applicable individual and caregiver understanding of medical treatment options.section 299b–36(c)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: x
children.text: Allowing States to test and evaluate fully integrating care for dual eligible individuals in the State, including the management and oversight of all funds under the applicable subchapters with respect to such individuals.

children.label: xi
children.text: Allowing States to test and evaluate systems of all-payer payment reform for the medical care of residents of the State, including dual eligible individuals.

children.label: xii
children.text: Aligning nationally recognized, evidence-based guidelines of cancer care with payment incentives under subchapter XVIII in the areas of treatment planning and follow-up care planning for applicable individuals described in clause (i) or (iii) of subsection (a)(4)(A) with cancer, including the identification of gaps in applicable quality measures.

children.label: xiii
children.text: Improving post-acute care through continuing care hospitals that offer inpatient rehabilitation, long-term care hospitals, and home health or skilled nursing care during an inpatient stay and the 30 days immediately following discharge.

children.label: xiv
children.text: Funding home health providers who offer chronic care management services to applicable individuals in cooperation with interdisciplinary teams.

children.label: I
children.text: developing, documenting, and disseminating best practices and proven care methods;

children.label: II
children.text: implementing such best practices and proven care methods within such institutions to demonstrate further improvements in quality and efficiency; and

children.label: III
children.text: providing assistance to other health care institutions on how best to employ such best practices and proven care methods to improve health care quality and lower costs.

children.label: xvi
children.text: Facilitate inpatient care, including intensive care, of hospitalized applicable individuals at their local hospital through the use of electronic monitoring by specialists, including intensivists and critical care specialists, based at integrated health systems.

children.label: xvii
children.text: Promoting greater efficiencies and timely access to outpatient services (such as outpatient physical therapy services) through models that do not require a physician or other health professional to refer the service or be involved in establishing the plan of care for the service, when such service is furnished by a health professional who has the authority to furnish the service under existing State law.

children.label: xviii
children.text: Establishing comprehensive payments to Healthcare Innovation Zones, consisting of groups of providers that include a teaching hospital, physicians, and other clinical entities, that, through their structure, operations, and joint-activity deliver a full spectrum of integrated and comprehensive health care services to applicable individuals while also incorporating innovative methods for the clinical training of future health care professionals.

children.label: I
children.text: in treating behavioral health issues (such as post-traumatic stress disorder) and stroke; and

children.label: II
children.text: to improve the capacity of non-medical providers and non-specialized medical providers to provide health services for patients with chronic complex conditions.

children.label: xx
children.text: Utilizing a diverse network of providers of services and suppliers to improve care coordination for applicable individuals described in subsection (a)(4)(A)(i) with 2 or more chronic conditions and a history of prior-year hospitalization through interventions developed under the Medicare Coordinated Care Demonstration Project under section 4016 of the Balanced Budget Act of 1997 ( note).42 U.S.C. 1395b–1

children.label: xxi
children.text: Focusing primarily on physicians’ services (as defined in ) furnished by physicians who are not primary care practitioners.section 1395w–4(j)(3) of this title

children.label: xxii
children.text: Focusing on practices of 15 or fewer professionals.

children.label: xxiii
children.text: Focusing on risk-based models for small physician practices which may involve two-sided risk and prospective patient assignment, and which examine risk-adjusted decreases in mortality rates, hospital readmissions rates, and other relevant and appropriate clinical measures.

children.label: xxiv
children.text: Focusing primarily on subchapter XIX, working in conjunction with the Center for Medicaid and CHIP Services.

children.label: xxv
children.text: Providing, for the adoption and use of certified EHR technology (as defined in section 1395w–4()(4) of this title) to improve the quality and coordination of care through the electronic documentation and exchange of health information, incentive payments to behavioral health providers (such as psychiatric hospitals (as defined in ), community mental health centers (as defined in ), hospitals that participate in a State plan under subchapter XIX or a waiver of such plan, treatment facilities that participate in such a State plan or such a waiver, mental health or substance use disorder providers that participate in such a State plan or such a waiver, clinical psychologists (as defined in ), nurse practitioners (as defined in ) with respect to the provision of psychiatric services, and clinical social workers (as defined in )).osection 1395x(f) of this titlesection 1395x(ff)(3)(B) of this titlesection 1395x(ii) of this titlesection 1395x(aa)(5) of this titlesection 1395x(hh)(1) of this title

children.label: xxvi
children.text: Supporting ways to familiarize individuals with the availability of coverage under part B of subchapter XVIII for qualified psychologist services (as defined in ).section 1395x(ii) of this title

children.label: xxvii
children.text: Exploring ways to avoid unnecessary hospitalizations or emergency department visits for mental and behavioral health services (such as for treating depression) through use of a 24-hour, 7-day a week help line that may inform individuals about the availability of treatment options, including the availability of qualified psychologist services (as defined in ).section 1395x(ii) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Whether the model includes a regular process for monitoring and updating patient care plans in a manner that is consistent with the needs and preferences of applicable individuals.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the model places the applicable individual, including family members and other informal caregivers of the applicable individual, at the center of the care team of the applicable individual.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the model provides for in-person contact with applicable individuals.

children.label: iv
children.text: Whether the model utilizes technology, such as electronic health records and patient-based remote monitoring systems, to coordinate care over time and across settings.

children.label: v
children.text: Whether the model provides for the maintenance of a close relationship between care coordinators, primary care practitioners, specialist physicians, community-based organizations, and other providers of services and suppliers.

children.label: vi
children.text: Whether the model relies on a team-based approach to interventions, such as comprehensive care assessments, care planning, and self-management coaching.

children.label: vii
children.text: Whether, under the model, providers of services and suppliers are able to share information with patients, caregivers, and other providers of services and suppliers on a real time basis.

children.label: viii
children.text: Whether the model demonstrates effective linkage with other public sector payers, private sector payers, or statewide payment models.

children.heading: Initial period
children.label: A Initial period
children.text: The Secretary shall not require, as a condition for testing a model under paragraph (1), that the design of such model ensure that such model is budget neutral initially with respect to expenditures under the applicable subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: improve the quality of care (as determined by the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) without increasing spending under the applicable subchapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: reduce spending under the applicable subchapter without reducing the quality of care; or

children.label: iii
children.text: improve the quality of care and reduce spending.

children.label: i
children.text: the quality of care furnished under the model, including the measurement of patient-level outcomes and patient-centeredness criteria determined appropriate by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the changes in spending under the applicable subchapters by reason of the model.

children.heading: Information
children.label: B Information
children.text: The Secretary shall make the results of each evaluation under this paragraph available to the public in a timely fashion and may establish requirements for States and other entities participating in the testing of models under this section to collect and report information that the Secretary determines is necessary to monitor and evaluate such models.

children.heading: Measure selection
children.label: C Measure selection
children.text: To the extent feasible, the Secretary shall select measures under this paragraph that reflect national priorities for quality improvement and patient-centered care consistent with the measures described in 1395aaa(b)(7)(B) of this title.2So in original. Probably should be “in section”.2

children.label: A
children.text: reduce spending under applicable subchapter without reducing the quality of care; or3So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.3

children.label: B
children.text: improve the quality of patient care without increasing spending;

the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services certifies that such expansion would reduce (or would not result in any increase in) net program spending under applicable subchapters; and
the Secretary determines that such expansion would not deny or limit the coverage or provision of benefits under the applicable subchapter for applicable individuals.

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: 1 Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary may waive such requirements of subchapters XI and XVIII and of sections 1396a(a)(1), 1396a(a)(13), 1396b(m)(2)(A)(iii), and 1396u–4 (other than subsections (b)(1)(A) and (c)(5) of such section) of this title as may be necessary solely for purposes of carrying out this section with respect to testing models described in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: the selection of models for testing or expansion under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the selection of organizations, sites, or participants to test those models selected;

children.label: C
children.text: the elements, parameters, scope, and duration of such models for testing or dissemination;

children.label: D
children.text: determinations regarding budget neutrality under subsection (b)(3);

children.label: E
children.text: the termination or modification of the design and implementation of a model under subsection (b)(3)(B); and

children.label: F
children.text: determinations about expansion of the duration and scope of a model under subsection (c), including the determination that a model is not expected to meet criteria described in paragraph (1) or (2) of such subsection.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the testing and evaluation of models or expansion of such models under this section.

children.heading: Application to CHIP
children.label: e Application to CHIP
children.text: The Center may carry out activities under this section with respect to subchapter XXI in the same manner as provided under this section with respect to the program under the applicable subchapters.

children.label: A
children.text: $5,000,000 for the design, implementation, and evaluation of models under subsection (b) for fiscal year 2010;

children.label: B
children.text: $10,000,000,000 for the activities initiated under this section for the period of fiscal years 2011 through 2019; and

children.label: C
children.text: the amount described in subparagraph (B) for the activities initiated under this section for each subsequent 10-year fiscal period (beginning with the 10-year fiscal period beginning with fiscal year 2020).

children.heading: Use of certain funds
children.label: 2 Use of certain funds
children.text: Out of amounts appropriated under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (1), not less than $25,000,000 shall be made available each such fiscal year to design, implement, and evaluate models under subsection (b).

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: g Report to Congress
children.text: Beginning in 2012, and not less than once every other year thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on activities under this section. Each such report shall describe the models tested under subsection (b), including the number of individuals described in subsection (a)(4)(A)(i) and of individuals described in subsection (a)(4)(A)(ii) participating in such models and payments made under applicable subchapters for services on behalf of such individuals, any models chosen for expansion under subsection (c), and the results from evaluations under subsection (b)(4). In addition, each such report shall provide such recommendations as the Secretary determines are appropriate for legislative action to facilitate the development and expansion of successful payment models.
